module_mood_contract: Satisfied compounding — it runs while I sleep. Student leaves feeling the memory is real, alive, and theirs — not a demo artifact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.

rules_evaluated.evidence: M2 mood contract 'satisfied compounding — it runs while I sleep' is identified via the maintainer block (strategy doc not preloaded; fallback used per dispatch instructions). The file honors it: Phase 2 close ('Quietly, you just made a reusable capability. Same agent, next week's task.'), Phase 3 close ('Chat literally cannot do this.'), Close section ('the move you'll repeat at your desk on Monday, and every Monday after.'). All beats name the repeating, compounding nature without resolving into awe (M8 territory). Trace mood scores: Phase 1=8, Phase 2=9, Phase 3=8, Phase 4=8, Close=9.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room. No authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No authored conversation cues found. Grep for banned phrase list (share with neighbor, sitting next to you, tell the room, say aloud, take a moment, pause and reflect, breathe, sit with, let it land) returned only context-irrelevant matches inside fenced prompt blocks or inside direct-quote strings describing what Claude will do — not student-facing room-management instructions.

Phase scores: [8, 9, 8, 8], close: 9. All at or above 8. Phase 4 recovered from prior-trace 7 — the removal of the redundant body sentence (which had duplicated the prompt's own lowest-confidence ask) eliminates the structural friction that was stealing half a point.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — this module's arc doesn't preempt M3.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Close ends on 'the system will do more next Monday' — satisfaction and agency, not certainty or delegation-readiness. M3 unease (what Claude does without me watching) is not resolved or pre-empted here. Phase 1 Beat 2 explicitly defers M4 territory: 'Module 4 is the proper conversation about classification, data boundaries.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands reliably across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 3 compound move ('pages get sharper, not longer') is encoded in the fenced prompt with explicit forcing-function language: 'tell me three pages that got sharper (not longer) and one claim that got dropped or replaced.' Persona traces through it cleanly; the push-back move ('Show me the before and after') is earned, not accidental. The teaching moment is structurally inescapable — prompt demands the comparison, student reads the result.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — module's Big Idea is honored.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise file has no standalone Big Idea section (correct — that lives in the module file). The M2 Big Idea ('persistence + automation = compound system') is honored throughout and explicitly synthesized in the What happens + The point sections: 'Two things combined make a system: persistence (it remembers) and automation (it runs). Either alone is a toy. Together they compound.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Close: 'Every module after this one uses the memory you just built.' Sets up M3 (agent reads the memory and executes autonomously) cleanly without pre-empting M3's teaching moment about what happens when you hand off without watching.

No punitive framing. 'soft-pages.md' list names pages to sharpen, not mistakes. Phase 4 ('Some will be right. Some will miss. Reject those.') is neutral-epistemic. Phase 1 policy callout ('Always comply with your company's AI policy') is a discipline callout, not a shame threat.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 opens with curation discipline ('A memory is only as good as what goes into it.'). Phase 2: 'A library without a librarian is a cost. Give it one.' Phase 3: 'A dumb knowledge base grows. A good one sharpens.' All disciplinary leads, not failure-mode leads.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test — student leaves the chair while agent works.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 Beat 2: 'Agent does the heavy lifting; you don't copy-paste.' Beat 3 auto-mode approval creates a multi-file write the student watches rather than drives. Phase 3 integration runs without the student copying content. Close query runs through the built agent with citation output the student reads.

Beat 3 plan-mode block (lines 63-68) names option 4 as the forced-engagement path with explicit one-sentence feedback mechanic. Phase 4 approval: 'Some will be right. Some will miss. Reject those.' — rejection expectation baked in, not bare approval gate.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Self-report inflation — verify at artifact; over-flag for self-audit.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 3 body: 'Push back if a claim got sharper but the top didn't actually change.' — artifact verification, not summary trust. Phase 1 self-audit prompt: 'Assume at least one of the three has a generic top claim.' — over-flag framing applied.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise file. No lectures to place.
